Indicator Name: Number of connections to water services
Definition: Number of connections to water services during the reporting period.
Guidance: New connections can be obtained by subtracting the previous number of connections from the value at the end of the current reporting period. This metric can be disaggregated by user type (e.g., underserved, residential, non-residential).

Indicator Name: Number of connections to wastewater services
Definition: Number of new residential connections resulting from the project – if relevant including to underserved groups e.g. low income, previously excluded, rural populations (households) receiving water distribution services in a given year.
Number of hours of water supply per day from the project.
Water pressure measured by the time it takes to fill a liter of water.
Guidance: This metric is measured as a comparison of the water pressure before and after project implementation.

Definition: Percentage of water effectively delivered by the supplier/distribution company but not paid for by the end users. (water paid for by consumer / water delivered to consumer).
Percentage of physical water losses during transmission and distribution.
Guidance: This metric captures the difference between the amount of water supplied and the amount of water delivered to the end users.

Indicator Name: Number of Passengers Using the Transport Infrastructure
Definition: Number of passengers using a transportation service/infrastructure (roads, railway, airport, ports, etc.) during the reporting period.
Daily traffic measured in terms of vehicles, trains, flights, etc. per year (or other relevant time period).
Guidance: The utilization level may also be expressed for varying time periods (utilization per day, month, etc.).

Definition: Length of infrastructure incorporating and/or dedicated for non-motorized forms of transport (for example cycling paths/lanes).
Annual average toll price per km for heavy / light vehicles during the reporting period.
Tariff per ton of freight hauled during the reporting period.
Guidance: This can be expressed as average during the reporting period or value at a specific date (e.g., last day of the reporting period). Sub-sector should be specified (road, air, maritime, etc.) and there should not be aggregation across sectors.

Definition: Number of twenty-foot equivalent units of cargo containers transported/handled through the road/railway/port/airport during the reporting period.
Number of tons of bulk cargo transported/handled through the road/railway/port/airport during the reporting period.
Number of berth moves per hour during the reporting period.
Average vehicle, aircraft, or ship turnaround time during the reporting period.

Indicator Name: Number of Women in Senior Management
Definition: Number of FTE female employees in senior management roles at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Captures female representation in senior management; avoid double-counting across board/IC/other roles; collect total senior managers for %; see 2X Challenge benchmarks.

Indicator Name: Number of Women on the Board
Definition: Number of women on the Board of Directors at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Captures female representation on governing bodies; avoid double-counting; collect total board members for %; applies to FI boards, GP boards, and investees.

Indicator Name: Number of Investment Committee Members who are Female
Definition: Number of female voting members of the Investment Committee of a Debt/Equity Fund or FI.
Guidance: Captures female representation on investment committees; avoid double-counting; collect total IC members for %; see 2X Challenge benchmarks.

Definition: Number of enterprises with an active female founder(s) retaining an active role at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Refer to 2X Challenge benchmarks; applies to direct equity, GPs, and fund investees.

Indicator Name: Female Direct Jobs Supported (Operations and Maintenance)
Definition: Number of FTE female workers working for the client company or project at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Refer to guidance for Direct Jobs Supported (O&M); includes direct hires and third‑party hires; includes seasonal/contract/part‑time FTEs.

Definition: Number of temporary FTE female workers hired for construction of project assets during reporting period.
Guidance: Refer to guidance for Construction Jobs (Temporary Construction).

Indicator Name: Female Direct Jobs Created by the Investment (Operations and Maintenance)
Definition: Number of new FTE female workers hired as a direct consequence of the investment project during the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes direct and third‑party hires; includes seasonal/contract/part‑time FTEs; excludes pre‑existing jobs unless base case shows they would end.

Definition: Number of FTE workers working for the client company or project at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Extensive FTE conversion guidance provided (seasonal, part‑time, piece‑rate, variable hours, day laborers).

Definition: Number of temporary FTE workers hired for construction of project assets during reporting period.
Guidance: Includes all on‑site construction workers including third‑party; excludes workers of construction companies when client is itself a construction/real‑estate firm.

Indicator Name: Direct Jobs Created by the Investment (Operations and Maintenance)
Definition: Number of new FTE workers hired as a direct consequence of the investment project during the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes direct and third‑party hires; includes seasonal/contract/part‑time FTEs; extensive FTE conversion guidance provided.

Definition: Amount of GHG emissions expected or measured annually (tons CO2e/y).
Guidance: Report by GHG Protocol scopes; ex‑ante for expected emissions; ex‑post for actual; follow IFI GHG methodologies; see GHG Protocol tools.

Definition: Amount of CO2 sequestered during the reporting period.
Guidance: Use standards such as Verified Carbon Standard, Gold Standard, ACR, AFOLU, Plan Vivo; report separately from absolute emissions.

Definition: Amount of water consumed during the reporting period in cubic meters.
Guidance: May include evaporated, incorporated, or transferred water; can be annual or daily; used for efficiency metrics.

Definition: Amount of wastewater treated to appropriate standards during the reporting period.
Guidance: May include untreated discharges avoided.

Definition: Energy consumption measured in kWh or MJ during the reporting period.
Guidance: Distinguish heat vs electricity; can be used for performance metrics (e.g., kWh/m2).

Definition: Hectares of land used during the reporting period.
Guidance: Can be used for land‑use efficiency metrics (e.g., MW/ha).

Definition: Hectares of land under sustainable land management practices verified by recognized standards.
Guidance: Certification or independent audit may be used; methodologies differ for forestry, farmland, etc.

Definition: Amount of money spent by the client on activities benefiting local communities during the reporting period.
Guidance: Captures breadth of community contributions; excludes non‑financial contributions.

Definition: All transfers to government made by the client during the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes corporate taxes, sales taxes, VAT, royalties, dividends, concession fees, license fees, interest taxes, and other material payments.

Definition: Average agricultural yield (per hectare) reported by the client during the reporting period.
Guidance: This indicator assesses the general yield of an IFI’s client through its operations, including outgrower schemes. One way to calculate this indicator is aggregating yield per hectare of the client’s farmers.

Definition: Number of farmers linked to the client company as suppliers, buyers, contractors, or farming employees during the reporting period.
Gross value of sales of the product over the reporting period.
Volume of sales of the product over the reporting period.
Gross value of export sales of the product over the reporting period.
Volume of export sales of the product over the reporting period.
Total volume of the company's production during the reporting period.
Guidance: May be used for any type of production (crops, livestock, processed food, etc.). May be disaggregated into agricultural vs. industrial production. Count only final product volume, not intermediate products.

Indicator Name: Value of Purchases from Domestic Farmers
Definition: Value of the company’s purchases of raw materials from farmers during the reporting period.
Guidance: Specify product (e.g., wheat, rice, chicken) whenever feasible.

Indicator Name: Volume of Purchases from Domestic Farmers
Definition: Volume of the company’s purchases of raw materials from farmers during the reporting period.
Guidance: Specify product (e.g., wheat, rice, chicken) whenever feasible.

Definition: Number of students enrolled at the end of the reporting period, both full-time and part-time, where each discrete student is counted regardless of number of courses.
Number of female students enrolled at the end of the reporting period, both full-time and part-time, where each discrete student is counted regardless of number of courses.
Guidance: Practitioners will need to collect total 'Students Enrolled' to get a percentage. Applicability: Students of a client company in the Education sector.

Definition: Energy generated during the reporting period.
Guidance: Include footnote about energy type (renewables and non‑renewables).

Definition: Renewable energy generated during the reporting period.
Guidance: Institutions should mention the source of renewable energy generated, including waste, hydropower, wind, solar, geothermal, biomass, and other sources.

Indicator Name: Number of New Connections to Energy
Definition: Number of connections to energy services during the reporting period.
Guidance: New connections = current period connections minus previous period. Can be disaggregated by user type (underserved, residential, non‑residential).

Definition: Frequency of power outages in the project area measured as SAIFI (System Average Interruption Frequency Index).
Guidance: SAIFI = total number of customer interruptions (annually) / total number of customers served (annually).

Definition: Length of power outages in the project area measured as SAIDI (System Average Interruption Duration Index).
Guidance: SAIDI = sum of hours of customer interruptions (annually) / total number of customers served (annually).

Indicator Name: Power System Commercial Losses (% of output)
Definition: Percentage of non‑technical (commercial) losses for utility projects during the reporting period.
Guidance: Captures energy delivered but not paid for (theft, billing issues, lack of meters). Metric = commercial losses (currency) / total sales (currency).

Indicator Name: Power System Technical Losses (% of output)
Definition: Percentage of technical losses for utility projects during the reporting period.
Guidance: Captures energy generated but not delivered due to grid inefficiencies. Metric = energy generated (MWh) / energy effectively delivered (MWh).

Definition: Number of outstanding loans in the eligible financial intermediary institution portfolio at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Reflects number of eligible loans not fully repaid or written off. 'Outstanding' is a stock variable. Not intended to measure number of clients served. Can be disaggregated by sex, age, company size, sector, geography, etc. Applies to intermediated finance only.

Definition: Value of outstanding loans in the eligible financial intermediary institution's portfolio at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes value of all loans not fully repaid or written off. 'Outstanding' is a stock variable. Can be disaggregated by sex, age, company size, sector, geography, etc. Applies to intermediated finance only.

Definition: Number of unique clients with outstanding accounts at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Removes double counting from multiple loans to same client. Does not cover repeat loans across reporting periods. Can be disaggregated by sex, age, company size, sector, geography, etc.

Definition: Average (simple mean) initial tenor (in months) of all outstanding loans in the eligible portfolio.
Guidance: Simple average = sum of all tenors / number of loans. Can be disaggregated by sex, age, company size, sector, geography, etc. Applies to intermediated finance only.

Definition: Number of deposit/savings transaction accounts in the financial intermediary institution portfolio at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes accounts used for payments (checking/current). Includes mobile money/e‑money accounts. Can track active accounts (≥1 transaction/30 days).

Definition: Number of merchant acceptance points (point‑of‑sale payment solutions) at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes physical POS terminals, mobile POS, QR‑based solutions. May track active users or transaction growth.

Definition: Number of clients with access to digital payment services (card, internet payments, platform, app, etc.) at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Includes debit/credit cards, app‑based payments, platform‑based payments. May be provided by the deposit account institution or a third party.

Definition: Value of payments processed measured by Gross Transaction Value during the reporting period.
Guidance: May include all transactions processed by intermediary or be limited to non‑cash, digital merchant payments, or cross‑border payments.

Indicator Name: Number of Active Female and Women‑Owned/Led Enterprise Clients
Definition: Number of active female clients and/or women‑owned/led enterprise clients served by a financial institution.
Guidance: Requires verifying female ownership/leadership. May be disaggregated by portfolio type. Refers to 2X Challenge and IFC definitions.

Indicator Name: Number of Loans Outstanding to Female and Women‑Owned/Led Enterprise Clients
Definition: Number of outstanding loans to female and women‑owned/led enterprise clients at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Same guidance as FI‑01 plus gender‑specific verification. May be disaggregated by portfolio type. Refers to 2X Challenge and IFC definitions.

Indicator Name: Value of Loans Outstanding to Female and Women‑Owned/Led Enterprise Clients
Definition: Value of outstanding loans to female and women‑owned/led enterprise clients at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Same guidance as FI‑02 plus gender‑specific verification. May be disaggregated by portfolio type. Refers to 2X Challenge and IFC definitions.

Indicator Name: Number of Deposit Transaction Accounts to Female and Women‑Owned/Led Enterprise Clients
Definition: Number of deposit/savings transaction accounts held by female and women‑owned/led enterprise clients at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Same guidance as FI‑05 plus gender‑specific verification. May be disaggregated by portfolio type. Refers to 2X Challenge and IFC definitions.

Indicator Name: Access to Digital Payment Services for Female and Women‑Owned/Led Enterprise Clients
Definition: Number of female and women‑owned/led enterprise clients with access to digital payment services at the end of the reporting period.
Guidance: Same guidance as FI‑07 plus gender‑specific verification. May be disaggregated by portfolio type. Refers to 2X Challenge and IFC definitions.

Definition: Number of patient consultations provided by the client company during the reporting period.
Number of female patient consultations provided by the client company during the reporting period.
Guidance: Practitioners will need to collect total 'Patients Served' to get a percentage. Applicability: Patients of a client company in the Health sector.

Definition: Number of residential dwellings (≥1 family per unit) refurbished by the client company at the end of the reporting period.
Number of new residential dwellings (≥1 family per unit) constructed by the client company at the end of the reporting period.

Value of company’s purchases of goods and services from domestic suppliers (including raw materials, security, gardening, cleaning, and marketing & research from local companies) during the reporting period.
Value of export sales of the product or service over the reporting period.
Unit count (as applicable) and gross value of sales over the reporting period.
mobile service - individuals
Guidance: Number of personal connections at the end of the reporting period.

Definition: mobile service - firms
Guidance: Number of commercial connections at the end of the reporting period.

Definition: fixed broadband - individuals
Guidance: Number of personal connections at the end of the reporting period.

Definition: fixed broadband - firms
Guidance: Number of personal connections at the end of the reporting period.

Indicator Name: Number of new Customers with access to fixed broadband
Definition: Number of unique households/firms that gained access to fixed broadband during the reporting period, having had no prior access.
cloud services - firms
Guidance: Number of firms that contracted cloud services from the provider during the reporting period.

Definition: % of data center capacity sold.
% of data center capacity sold to wholesale customers.

Indicator Name: Percentage of population or households with access
Definition: Percentage of population/households that live in a location covered by the client's network during the reporting period.
Number of unique households/firms located in an area covered by the client's network during the reporting period.

Indicator Name: Amount of data center IT power / number of racks added
Definition: Data center capacity added by the entity during the reporting period.
Submarine cable data transmission capacity added during the reporting period.
Kilometers of new lines built/in service (long-haul/backhaul/metro, fiber optics or other technologies) during the reporting period.
Kilometers of lines upgraded (long-haul/backhaul/metro, fiber optics or other technologies) during the reporting period.
Number of new sites/cells (BTS/towers, antennas, etc.) built during the reporting period.
Number of sites/cells upgraded during the reporting period.
Average number of tenants (operators) per tower during the reporting period.
Median download speed of fixed/mobile broadband in the market.
Price of the cheapest mobile/fixed plan in the market.

Indicator Name: Number of subscribers to an affordable plan
Definition: Number of subscribers to mobile/fixed broadband plans costing <2% of average income.
Guidance: Average income must match the population of interest.

Indicator Name: Number of individuals with improved access to services due to a disruptive digital technology
Definition: Number of individuals with better access to services due to the introduction of a disruptive digital technology.
Guidance: Examples: AR/VR health or education services, AI-enabled services.

Indicator Name: Number of users of the disruptive digital technology
Definition: Number of individuals using the client’s disruptive digital technology during the reporting period.
Guidance: Examples: blockchain, AI, VR, cloud computing, IoT.

Indicator Name: Number of buyers of the disruptive digital technology
Definition: Number of individuals/firms buying a disruptive digital technology from the client during the reporting period.
Guidance: Examples: AR/VR, cloud computing, 3D printing, IoT.

Indicator Name: Number of service providers on the digital platform
Definition: Number of individuals/firms providing services on the digital platform.

Indicator Name: Payment to service providers on the digital platform
Definition: Total payment to individual service providers (drivers, teachers, gig workers, etc.) during the reporting period.

Indicator Name: Number of transactions on the digital platform
Definition: Number of completed transactions between buyers and sellers on the digital platform.

Indicator Name: Value of operational improvements due to the disruptive digital technology
Definition: Value of improvements in operational performance (cost savings, revenue growth, productivity gains) due to the technology.

Indicator Name: Number of firms reporting improved operational performance due to the technology
Definition: Number of firms reporting improved operational performance due to the introduction of a disruptive digital technology.

Indicator Name: Number of firms using the disruptive digital technology to innovate
Definition: Number of firms using the technology to introduce new products, business models, or standards.
Guidance: Innovation must be clearly linked to the technology.

Indicator Name: Number of individuals trained on the disruptive digital technology
Definition: Number of individuals trained on the disruptive digital technology during the reporting period.
Guidance: Examples: blockchain, AI, VR, cloud computing, IoT.

Definition: Volume of output (commodity or intermediate goods).
Guidance: Could be disaggregated by domestic use and exports.

Indicator Name: Volume of minerals/metals sourced from local/regional supplier(s)
Definition: Volume of minerals/metals sourced from local/regional supplier(s).
Volume of oil/gas delivered to end-customers during the reporting period.
Guidance: Could be disaggregated by domestic consumption and exports.

Definition: Number of gas distribution service connections during the reporting period.
Guidance: New connections = current period connections minus previous period. Can be disaggregated by user type (underserved, residential, non-residential). Applies only to gas distribution projects.

Definition: Number of Liquid Natural Gas (LNG) / Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) stations during the reporting period.
Length of service interruptions during the reporting period.

Indicator Name: Direct jobs supported in the investee company (Operations and Maintenance)
Definition: Number of FTE workers working for the investee company at end of reporting period, including direct hires and third-party hires; includes seasonal, contractual, and part‑time workers.
Guidance: Extensive FTE rules; excludes gig workers and contractors not employed by the company; report end‑period FTE unless strong seasonality.

Indicator Name: Female Direct jobs supported in the investee company (Operations and Maintenance)
Definition: Number of FTE female workers working for the investee company at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑01; same FTE rules; excludes gig workers; report end‑period FTE unless strong seasonality.

Definition: Number of active investments at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Count each investee once regardless of number of instruments; applies to funds, not intermediated finance.

Indicator Name: Number of Active Fund Investments in Female Founded Enterprises
Definition: Number of active fund investments in enterprises with active female founder(s).
Guidance: Female founder must retain active role; count each investee once; refer to 2X Challenge guidance.

Definition: Cumulative number of realized investments as of end of reporting period.
Guidance: Count each investee once; only count full exits; write‑offs count as realized.

Indicator Name: Number of realized Fund Investments in Female Founded Enterprises
Definition: Number of realized investments in female‑founded enterprises.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑05; female founder must have retained active role throughout investment.

Definition: Value of active invested capital (cost basis) at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Exclude fees/expenses; may report debt/equity separately or combined; distinct from valuation.

Indicator Name: Amount of active Fund Investments in Female Founded Enterprises
Definition: Value of active invested capital (cost basis) in female‑founded enterprises.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑07; female founder must retain active role; distinct from valuation.

Definition: Cumulative value of realized invested capital (cost basis) at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Includes write‑offs; does not reflect proceeds; may combine debt/equity if explicit.

Indicator Name: Amount of realized Fund Investments in Female Founded Enterprises
Definition: Cumulative value of realized invested capital (cost basis) in female‑founded enterprises.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑09; female founder must have retained active role throughout investment.

Definition: All transfers to government during reporting period (taxes, royalties, VAT, fees, dividends, etc.).
Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization.
Guidance: Follow international accounting standards; report in agreed currency.

Definition: Gross value of sales during the reporting period.
Guidance: Covers earned revenue only; excludes financial income or asset sales.

Definition: Value of company’s purchases of goods/services from domestic suppliers during reporting period.
Guidance: Typically not applicable to intermediated operations.

Definition: Value of export sales during reporting period.
Guidance: Typically not applicable to intermediated operations.

Indicator Name: Direct Jobs Supported in the Intermediary
Definition: Number of FTE workers working for the intermediary (the fund manager).
Guidance: Exclude investee company workers; extensive FTE rules; excludes outsourced services.

Indicator Name: Female Direct Jobs Supported in the Intermediary
Definition: Number of FTE female workers working for the intermediary.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑16; same FTE rules; excludes outsourced services.

Definition: Number of voting Investment Committee members at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Avoid double‑counting across roles; applies to funds only.

Indicator Name: Number of Investment Committee Members who are Female
Definition: Number of female voting Investment Committee members at end of reporting period.
Guidance: Subset of PE‑18; refer to 2X Challenge benchmarks.

Definition: The client adopts or operationalizes a product, internal process, technology, or financing structure that is new or not widely used in the domestic sector.
Guidance: Covers adoption of innovative business practices, including technology transfer and new financial or operational structures. Excludes supplier/client relationship innovations (covered under PF‑02).

Definition: The client improves existing or engages new forward or backward market linkages, including through its own integration.
Guidance: Tracks improved or new supply‑chain linkages that expand market access. Forward = downstream customers; backward = upstream suppliers. Distinct from PF‑01 (innovation).

Definition: The client expands operations and increases market share in a concentrated market where it is not a dominant player.
Guidance: Used when the client grows share in a concentrated market. Only one of PF‑03 or PF‑04 applies at a time. Distinct from new market entry.

Definition: The client enters a new market, defined by relevant country/sector/product/beneficiary context.
Guidance: Captures entry into a new market via establishment and commencement of sales. Mutually exclusive with PF‑03.

Definition: Additional private sector firms have entered the relevant market in which the client operates.
Guidance: Used only when PSD firm‑level competition indicators apply. Tracks increased competition but is not directly attributable to the client. Data typically sourced from client reporting or market research.

Definition: Percentage of customers with meters for electricity, gas, or water services.
Guidance: Metric = customers with meters / total customers.
